references.title: Algorithmic group formation and group work evaluation in a learning analytics-enhanced environment: implementation study in a Japanese junior high school

annotations.text: The authors propose a learning analytics framework for collaborative learning which they coin as Group Learning Orchestrated Based on Evidence (GLOBE) which requires algorithmic group formation. The authors test their framework on 12 collaborative learning activities - testing both homogenous and heterogenous group formation.

The groups formed algorithmically were shown to perform better than random grouping. Moreover, the peer satisfaction  and self-perception were higher for students in the homogenous groups for the idea exchange tasks. In the reading tasks, there were no significant differences.
